user_query:
I want a minimal, reproducible prototype that builds a 7-day hybrid yoga + strength training plan from authoritative sources, using the included skeleton. Please implement and wire up src/app.py so that running `python src/app.py` produces a complete pipeline that: (1) performs web searches to find two authoritative sources; (2) downloads and saves the raw HTML locally; (3) extracts structured data from those pages; (4) generates a weekly plan driven by the config; and (5) emits clearly defined outputs. Use only the Python standard library.

Authoritative Internet sources to use (no direct URLs in code):
- One .gov or .edu page that describes resistance/strength training movement patterns and/or gives named example exercises. Use a general search engine and record the exact query you used; target a government or academic domain.
- One .org page from a recognized yoga organization that lists yoga poses with categories and difficulty/level tags. Use a general search engine and record the exact query you used.

Save the raw pages to local files so the process is locally verifiable:
- downloads/strength_source_1.html (the .gov or .edu page)
- downloads/yoga_source_1.html (the .org yoga pose page)

Extract structured data from the saved HTML (do not hardcode content; parse headings/lists/tables as available on your chosen pages):
- data/strength_movements.json: an array of objects with fields {"source_url", "movement_category", "exercise_name"}. Ensure at least 8 entries total across at least 3 distinct movement_category values (e.g., push, pull, squat, hinge, core—names based on what the source calls them).
- data/yoga_poses.json: an array of objects with fields {"source_url", "name", "category", "difficulty"}. Ensure at least 12 entries with at least 3 distinct categories and at least 2 distinct difficulty/level values.

Generate the weekly plan according to input/config.yaml (you may edit code and use the provided config values):
- outputs/weekly_plan.json: a JSON object keyed by day number strings "1".."7". Each active day must include segments with structure {"type": "yoga"|"strength"|"rest", "duration_minutes", "details": {...}}. For yoga segments, include details.pose_names (list of ≥2 pose names that must match entries in data/yoga_poses.json) and details.styles (subset drawn from config preferences). For strength segments, include details.movement_category and details.exercise_names (list of ≥2 names that must match entries in data/strength_movements.json). Rest days should have a single segment of type "rest" with duration 0.
- outputs/compliance_report.json: {"total_sessions", "total_minutes", "yoga_minutes", "strength_minutes", "yoga_styles_used", "strength_patterns_covered", "constraints_met" (boolean), "violations" (array of strings)}. Use config values to verify: (a) 7 total days with exactly 4 active days; (b) per-day session length approximates config.session_length_minutes with yoga:strength time split near 60:40 (±2 minutes per segment is acceptable); (c) do not schedule the same yoga category on consecutive active days; (d) use at least two distinct yoga styles across the week; (e) cover at least four of the five strength patterns (push, pull, squat, hinge, core) across active days.
- outputs/sources.json: {"search_queries": {"yoga": "...", "strength": "..."}, "sources": [{"type": "yoga"|"strength", "url", "saved_path", "sha256", "retrieved_at"}]} where sha256 is the hash of the saved HTML file and retrieved_at is ISO8601.

Additional requirements:
- Read parameters from input/config.yaml. Keep all outputs under the specified directories. Do not rely on live page structure being identical across runs; implement robust, minimal parsing that extracts names/categories/levels from the saved HTML content you download. Do not embed or hardcode external content into the repo; always parse from the downloaded files.
- All generated yoga/strength segment names in outputs/weekly_plan.json must be present in the corresponding data/*.json files; the plan should reference them by exact string match for cross-validation.
- Use only standard library modules. Do not add external dependencies. Your deliverable is a runnable src/app.py that creates the downloads, data, and outputs artifacts from scratch using the config settings provided.

Paths and formats to produce:
- downloads/strength_source_1.html, downloads/yoga_source_1.html
- data/strength_movements.json, data/yoga_poses.json
- outputs/weekly_plan.json, outputs/compliance_report.json, outputs/sources.json

I’m a yoga practitioner who believes in blending yoga and strength for optimal fitness. This prototype is for my personal use and future iteration. Please keep the code clear and small, but complete enough to run end-to-end with `python src/app.py`. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
